title: Source lineage for Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (Böddeker’s 45-text selection)
summary: The pinned repository XML is a version of U-M’s 2006 uncorrected OCR, created from a historically reported 484-image scan of Böddeker’s complete 1878 edition. The cataloged body is Böddeker’s exact pp. 95-300 selection of forty-five discontiguous Harley 2253 works, which he transcribed and edited directly from the manuscript while consulting ten modeled earlier editions item by item. Those printed intermediaries are lateral consulted/collated branches, not replacements for Böddeker’s direct Harley base. Three complete 1878 scan objects, the PIMS OCR, the ten prior editions’ complete digital carriers, a modern METS edition, and DIMEV v1 are cached as research controls. Current BL manuscript images are advertised but unavailable; U-M delivery is Cloudflare-challenged; Ker’s complete target-covering printed facsimile has only physical/catalog/commercial routes. Rights remain distinct for medieval wording (U.S. public domain, U.K. unresolved), every historical edition, each scan/OCR derivative, U-M encoding/images, Ker, METS, DIMEV, Brill, and the repository artifact.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for afy7793
entities.description: The exact XML artifact pinned through the CME submodule and cataloged by this manifest.

entities.label: University of Michigan OCR encoding AFY7793.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 U-M Library uncorrected OCR text encoded at TEI in Libraries Level 1. The body retains Böddeker’s forty-five-text pp. 95-300 selection; much surrounding print matter is represented only by page-break placeholders.
entities.reproduction_format: Uncorrected OCR XML with structural markup and historical page-image links

entities.label: University of Michigan 484-image digital facsimile for AFY7793.0001.001
entities.description: The page-image layer named in the U-M header as 484 600 dpi TIFF G4 images linked to the OCR. Its current image delivery route could not be verified through the Cloudflare-challenged resolver; the Internet Archive item bearing the identifier contains metadata only.
entities.reproduction_format: 484 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images (historically reported)

entities.label: Karl Böddeker, Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (1878)
alternate_labels: Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253: Mit Grammatik und Glossar
entities.description: Böddeker’s printed edition of Harley 2253 material. The exact afy7793 cataloging subject is its forty-five-text selected section on pp. 95-300, not the whole book.

bibliographic.citation: Karl Böddeker, ed., Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253: Mit Grammatik und Glossar (Berlin: Weidmannsche Buchhandlung, 1878), xvi, 463, [1] pp.
bibliographic.title: Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253: Mit Grammatik und Glossar

notes: The afy7793 selection is printed pp. 95-300.
The complete volume also contains introduction, grammar, glossary, appendix, and corrections outside the target boundary.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2253
entities.description: Composite parchment codex. The relevant second part, ff. 49r-142v, was copied principally by the Harley scribe near Ludlow circa 1330-1340; METS narrows the main phase to approximately 1331-1341. Böddeker selects forty-five discontiguous Middle English works within ff. 55v-128v.

date_statements.label: relevant manuscript part
date_statements.notes: This dates the witness component, not every selected text’s composition.

date_statements.label: main Harley-scribe copying phase
date_statements.notes: METS scholarly framing; retained alongside the official catalog’s broader range.

physical_description.extent: 1* + 142 folios plus flyleaves; relevant second part ff. 49r-142v
notes: The selected works are interspersed with unselected Middle English, Anglo-Norman, and Latin material.

entities.label: British Library digital facsimile of Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The current official catalog reports full digital coverage but says that digital images are currently unavailable. This entity records the unavailable image layer without inventing an undocumented IIIF endpoint.
entities.reproduction_format: Cataloged complete manuscript page-image coverage; current delivery unavailable

entities.label: Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253 (EETS Original Series 255)
entities.description: N. R. Ker’s 1965 monochrome printed partial facsimile. Its Harley portion, ff. 49r-140v and 142r-v, fully covers every afy7793 target folio but not the physical codex as a whole.

bibliographic.citation: N. R. Ker, ed., Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253, Early English Text Society, Original Series 255 (London and New York: Oxford University Press for EETS, 1965), xxiii, [194] pp.
bibliographic.title: Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253
bibliographic.series: Early English Text Society, Original Series 255
bibliographic.publisher: Oxford University Press for the Early English Text Society

notes: Reproduces Harley 2253 ff. 49r-140v and 142r-v plus Royal 12 C XII ff. 68v and 76v.
All afy7793 target folios are within the reproduced Harley portion; the two Royal folios have no target overlap.

entities.label: Internet Archive/PIMS complete scan of Böddeker 1878
entities.description: Complete public scan of a PIMS-University of Toronto copy of Böddeker’s 1878 volume. The target occupies printed pp. 95-300 / PDF pages 115-320.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete downloadable PDF scan with scan-data sidecar

entities.label: Internet Archive OCR for the PIMS Böddeker scan
entities.description: Complete plain-text OCR derivative of the PIMS scan. It contains the target selection but is used as a search/control source, not as an authority over the page images or repository XML.

entities.label: Internet Archive Oxford/Google complete scan of Böddeker 1878
entities.description: Complete scan of an Oxford University copy digitized by Google and distributed through Internet Archive. The target is visually confirmed at PDF pages 117-322.

entities.label: Internet Archive BWB complete scan of Böddeker 1878
entities.description: A third complete public scan of Böddeker’s 1878 volume. The target is visually confirmed at PDF pages 117-322. The exact source-library expansion of the BWB identifier is not inferred.

entities.label: The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript
entities.description: Later complete scholarly edition and translation of the manuscript. Its exact forty-five corresponding articles provide a modern item, folio, genre, and textual control but are not upstream of afy7793.

date_statements.label: publication
date_statements.notes: Volume 2 appeared in 2014; volumes 1 and 3 in 2015.

entities.reproduction_format: Digital scholarly edition, English translations, and combined downloadable PDF

entities.description: Earlier critical edition whose variant apparatus Böddeker used because the Auchinleck and Digby copies were inaccessible to him; Böddeker explicitly adopted some of Mall’s judgments.

access.contact: British Library Imaging Services; quote Harley MS 2253 and specify every requested surface recto/verso
access.format: Reference or reproduction photography; manuscript originals are photographed, with microfilm used where available unless color/original photography is required
notes: Publication or reuse may require separate written permission and fees; rights clearance remains the requester’s responsibility.

rights.component: Pinned repository XML artifact
rights.basis: The CME repository LICENSE expressly dedicates “software” under the Unlicense; it does not clearly grant rights in inherited OCR/XML textual content. No source-file-specific license was found.

rights.component: U-M OCR, markup, and digital-text presentation
rights.basis: The embedded 2006 availability notice says copyright may apply and other distribution restrictions may apply. Current item-specific rights text could not be verified through the challenged resolver.

rights.component: U-M page-image files and delivery
rights.basis: The header reports 484 images but supplies no image-specific open license; current image delivery and rights labels could not be verified. Public-domain print content does not by itself determine terms for hosted image files.

rights.component: Böddeker’s 1878 text, selection, commentary, and printed edition
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1878. The U.S. Copyright Office states that copyright in works published before January 1, 1931 has expired and such works are in the public domain. This conclusion does not automatically cover later scan files or provider contracts.

rights.component: Underlying medieval wording of the forty-five selected texts
rights.basis: The selected wording was fixed in a fourteenth-century witness and is far outside the U.S. copyright term. This does not determine rights in modern transcriptions, editions, photographs, or metadata.

rights.component: Any current or restored British Library digital images
rights.basis: The official record currently exposes no image object or item-specific image-rights statement. British Library general and imaging terms require source-specific checking and may require written permission for reproduction.

rights.component: Ker introduction, photographic reproductions, selection, and printed layout
rights.jurisdiction: United States and applicable publication jurisdictions
rights.basis: The 1965 facsimile and editorial introduction may remain protected; no open license or conclusive public-domain determination was found. Underlying medieval wording does not settle rights in the photographs or editorial matter.

rights.component: PIMS/Internet Archive PDF scan and scan metadata
rights.basis: The underlying 1878 edition is public domain in the United States, but the IA item supplies no item-specific license for the scan derivative or metadata. Availability is recorded separately from reuse permission.

rights.component: Internet Archive OCR derivative
rights.basis: No item-specific license is supplied for the OCR derivative. The public-domain status of the underlying edition does not by itself state provider terms for the generated file.

rights.component: Oxford/Google/Internet Archive PDF scan
rights.basis: The IA item exposes a free download but no item-specific license. Rights or contract terms for the Google-origin scan are not inferred from the 1878 edition’s U.S. public-domain status.

rights.component: BWB/Internet Archive PDF scan
rights.basis: The IA item exposes a free download but no item-specific license. The exact source-library terms are not inferred from the identifier.

rights.component: METS modern edition, translations, and combined PDF
rights.basis: METS states that digital editions are for individual use and requires contact to reprint digital text. Its separate CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 statement applies to XML and is not automatically transferred to the cached combined PDF.

rights.component: DIMEV version 1.0 dataset, documentation, and metadata
rights.basis: The Zenodo record, repository, README, and embedded LICENSE identify Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

rights.component: Brill reprint, digitization, e-book file, and platform delivery
rights.basis: Brill identifies a 1969 copyright year and distributes the 2024 e-book commercially. The public-domain status of Böddeker’s 1878 content does not remove rights or contract terms in the later edition, digitization, or platform.

rights.component: Underlying wording of the forty-five selected medieval texts
rights.basis: The texts’ medieval age alone does not settle the United Kingdom position. Current Intellectual Property Office guidance explains that some pre-1989 unpublished literary works can remain protected through 31 December 2039; the valid publication or public-availability history has not been established item by item.

rights.component: Wright’s 1839 text, editorial matter, selection, and print edition

editorial_practices.source_feature: A multilingual manuscript miscellany with texts in codicological order
editorial_practices.representation: Forty-five selected Middle English works arranged as political, secular, religious, and five longer/standalone units
editorial_practices.description: Böddeker selected forty-five discontiguous Harley works and reordered them thematically. Printed adjacency therefore cannot be used as manuscript adjacency.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 95-300; complete afy7793 cataloging subject

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley manuscript readings, earlier printed editions, and parallel textual traditions
editorial_practices.representation: Edited text with item-specific emendations, comparisons, and textual notes
editorial_practices.description: Böddeker transcribed Harley directly but cited many prior editions and occasionally preferred an earlier editor’s reading. These interventions are item-specific and do not make one earlier edition the common source of the selection.
editorial_practices.scope: The forty-five selected texts and their interleaved apparatus
notes: Böddeker says he did not inspect Digby 86 directly; E. Stengel mediated the comparison, discovered after the selected text had already been printed, and the resulting appendix is outside afy7793.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 1878 volume with introduction, grammar, selected texts, glossary, appendix, and corrections
editorial_practices.representation: Complete target pp. 95-300 text; title page; surrounding print matter largely reduced to page-break placeholders
editorial_practices.description: The electronic encoding retains the entire forty-five-text target selection but omits most textual content outside it, even while preserving page milestones linked historically to the scan sequence.
editorial_practices.scope: FRONT, BODY, and BACK of afy7793.xml

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME source, afy7793.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY; BACK
evidence.summary: Exact artifact evidence for the U-M identifier and date, Böddeker source, 484-image statement, uncorrected OCR policy, Level 1 encoding, omitted surrounding matter, complete forty-five-text body, and page links.

evidence.citation: Kyvero Press CME repository LICENSE (Unlicense text limited expressly to software).
evidence.locator: Complete license text
evidence.summary: The repository license repeatedly identifies its subject as software; it is not treated as an item-specific grant for inherited OCR/XML textual content.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan stable resolver for AFY7793.0001.001.
evidence.locator: HTTP redirect and current access response
evidence.summary: The stable name resolver redirects to U-M’s idresolver; automated checks on 2026-07-15 received a Cloudflare challenge (HTTP 403), so current full-text and image availability and rights labels remain unverified.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata-only item afy7793.0001.001.umich.edu.
evidence.locator: Complete 681-byte metadata XML
evidence.summary: Labels the noindex item “Unprocessed book from the University of Michigan” and supplies identifiers but no target text, scan, or image derivatives.

evidence.citation: Karl Böddeker, Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (Berlin: Weidmann, 1878), complete PIMS/Internet Archive PDF, OCR, and scan-data.
evidence.locator: Title page; introduction and source discussion; contents; printed pp. 93-301; appendix; corrections
evidence.summary: Direct evidence for publication details, complete-volume boundary, exact pp. 95-300 target, thematic ordering, forty-five texts, German apparatus, direct Harley use, earlier-edition collation, and omitted surrounding matter.
notes: Companion OCR SHA-256 d1492a39d59dd4429efdd9c10378752bdf5c24c69e833eebdf638e7bd6c3eccd; scan-data SHA-256 be1ba086eb776289e1c9b947e7c485af1961b092cabd6bd635b72fd43b93d336.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for altenglischedich00boed.
evidence.locator: Item identity, files, contributor, sponsor, and rights/license fields
evidence.summary: Identifies the complete Böddeker scan from PIMS-University of Toronto and its public derivatives. No item-specific rights or license field is supplied.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item altenglischedic00dichgoog, Oxford/Google scan of Böddeker 1878.
evidence.locator: Complete 492-page PDF; target at PDF pp. 117-322
evidence.summary: Independent complete scan used to confirm target-page alignment and scan legibility. Metadata identifies Oxford University and Google source CUoCAAAAQAAJ but provides no item-specific license.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item bwb_O6-CTY-700, complete scan of Böddeker 1878.
evidence.locator: Complete 492-page PDF; target at PDF pp. 117-322
evidence.summary: Third complete scan used to confirm target-page alignment and content. The item metadata supplies no license and does not justify expanding the BWB identifier into an unverified institution name.

evidence.citation: Indiana University Libraries, IUCAT record 968050, Altenglische Dichtungen des Ms. Harl. 2253.
evidence.locator: Bibliographic description
evidence.summary: Confirms Berlin: Weidmann, 1878, xvi + 463-page bibliographic description, Böddeker editorial responsibility, and Harley 2253 manuscript association.

evidence.citation: Google Play Books, Altenglische Dichtungen des Ms. Harl. 2253, ID 3hQJAAAAQAAJ.
evidence.locator: Title, creator, publisher/date, page count, and current “Get for free” route
evidence.summary: Current no-cost provider route to the 1878 volume; recorded as access, not as proof of unrestricted scan-file reuse rights.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253 parent record.
evidence.locator: Composite object, extent, dimensions, holding, access, digital-coverage statement, and references
evidence.summary: Establishes the complete physical codex, British Library holding, restricted access, overall description, and full digital coverage currently marked unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253, ff. 49r-142v child record.
evidence.locator: Relevant component contents, date, origin, languages, script, and Harley-scribe activity
evidence.summary: Establishes the relevant second component, circa 1330-1340 date, near-Ludlow context, Harley scribe active 1314-circa 1349, and exact content inventory encompassing all target folios.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitisation and Imaging Services.
evidence.locator: Manuscript order requirements, formats, original-vs-microfilm photography, costs, and permissions
evidence.summary: Current mediated route for ordering manuscript photography, requiring shelfmark/title and exact recto/verso surfaces; manuscripts are photographed, microfilm may be used unless original/color photography is specified, and fees apply.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026, together with general terms.
evidence.locator: Reuse, reproduction, permissions, and user rights-clearance obligations
evidence.summary: Copies supplied by Imaging Services cannot be reproduced without written permission; additional fees may apply, and the user remains responsible for rights clearance. General terms warn that item-specific status varies.

evidence.citation: Catalog records for N. R. Ker, Facsimile of British Museum MS. Harley 2253, EETS OS 255 (1965).
evidence.locator: Publication description, physical extent, reproduction scope, holdings, snippets, and purchase enquiry
evidence.summary: Confirms 1965 EETS publication, xxiii + [194] pages, Harley ff. 49r-140v and 142r-v plus two Royal leaves, and physical/request/purchase routes. Discovery found no lawful complete public digital copy.

evidence.citation: Susanna Fein, ed. and trans., with David Raybin and Jan Ziolkowski, The Complete Harley 2253 Manuscript, combined three-volume PDF.
evidence.locator: articles 21-23, 25, 25a, 27-36, 40-41, 43-53, 58, 60-69, 73-74, 81, 88-89, and 92-93; appendix of complete manuscript contents
evidence.summary: Current scholarly control for exact article titles, manuscript folios and order, form/genre distinctions, Harley-scribe context, and later transcriptions/translations of all forty-five selected works. It is collateral rather than upstream.

evidence.citation: Middle English Texts Series, Copyright & Citation.
evidence.locator: Copyright section
evidence.summary: States that digital editions are for individual use and reprinting requires contact; separately licenses METS XML under CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. The XML license is not transferred to the cached PDF without evidence.

evidence.citation: Digital Index of Middle English Verse, version 1.0 dataset, Zenodo record 14585961.
evidence.summary: Open stable release used to independently confirm exactly forty-five target witness records, record IDs, incipits, descriptions, folio locators, Böddeker citations, and CC BY 4.0. A 2026 2.0-alpha.1 release exists but reports metadata/documentation changes; v1.0 remains the cited stable dataset.

evidence.citation: Brill, Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253, title 27831.
evidence.locator: Title, author/editor, copyright year, e-book format, ISBN, and access conditions
evidence.summary: Identifies the later commercial Brill reprint/e-book, copyright year 1969, e-book publication in 2024, ISBN 978-90-04-65070-1, and subscription/purchase route.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, “What Is Copyright?” and Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.locator: Public-domain and duration statements current in 2026
evidence.summary: The Copyright Office states that works published before January 1, 1931 are in the U.S. public domain because their terms have expired; used only for the 1878 edition and underlying medieval wording, not later source layers.

evidence.citation: Karl Böddeker, Altenglische Dichtungen des MS. Harl. 2253 (Berlin: Weidmann, 1878), item headnotes and textual apparatus for the selected pp. 95-300 section.
evidence.locator: Political-song headnotes/apparatus pp. 98-140; secular-song headnotes/apparatus pp. 145-179; religious-song headnotes/apparatus pp. 181-232; Body and Soul pp. 233-243; Harrowing of Hell pp. 264-284; Proverbs of Hendyng pp. 285-300
evidence.summary: Primary evidence that Böddeker consulted Wright 1839, Percy 1866, Ritson 1829, Mätzner 1867, Wright 1842, Morris-Skeat 1873, Wright-Halliwell 1845, Wright 1841, Mall 1871, and Kemble 1848 at the modeled item scopes. The apparatus distinguishes general citation from reading-level comparison; Mall supplied variants from manuscripts Böddeker explicitly says he could not access directly.
notes: The ten earlier editions are lateral consultation/collation intermediaries. They do not replace the direct edition:boeddeker:1878 transcribes witness:bl:Harley-2253 relation.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., The Political Songs of England, from the Reign of John to that of Edward II (London: Camden Society, 1839), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; target texts at printed pp. 69-71, 149-159, 187-194, 212-223, 237-240, and 246-250
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify edition identity, all eight political-song target overlaps, exact page boundaries, and the readings cited by Böddeker.

evidence.citation: Thomas Percy, Reliques of Ancient English Poetry, Tauchnitz Edition, vol. 2 (Leipzig: Bernhard Tauchnitz, 1866), complete Google/Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; Richard of Almaigne pp. 1-4; On the Death of King Edward the First pp. 5-8; embedded Google public-domain-book notice
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the edition and its two target texts. The PDF’s Google usage guidance is preserved as provider-term evidence rather than treated as a copyright license.
notes: qpdf succeeds with repetitive first-page-section, linearization, and hint-table shared-object warnings; the readable, text-extractable file is not classified as content-corrupt.

evidence.citation: Joseph Ritson, Ancient Songs and Ballads, 2nd ed., vol. 1 (London: Payne and Foss, 1829), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; exactly ten target items at printed pp. 12-14, 28-39, and 51-70, enumerated in access:ritson-scan
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the edition and exactly ten target overlaps, including three passages for which Böddeker records explicit Ritson readings.

evidence.citation: Eduard Mätzner, Altenglische Sprachproben, vol. 1, part 1, Poesie (Berlin: Weidmann, 1867), Bayerische Staatsbibliothek IIIF object bsb10747525.
evidence.locator: Embedded manifest metadata and NoC-NC rights URI; target/comparator passages at printed pp. 54-55 / canvases 62-63, pp. 90-103 / canvases 98-111, pp. 152-154 / canvases 160-162, and pp. 304-311 / canvases 312-319
evidence.summary: Complete 400-image IIIF bundle used to verify edition identity, four target/comparator overlaps, exact page/canvas mappings, and the provider’s NoC-NC rights statement.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., Specimens of Lyric Poetry, Composed in England in the Reign of Edward the First (London: Percy Society, 1842), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title and source statement; all fourteen target secular songs and eighteen target religious songs at their mapped starts between printed pp. 24 and 111
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the edition, all thirty-two target overlaps, the ordered start-page mapping, and the nine reading-level comparisons identified in Böddeker.

evidence.citation: Richard Morris and W. W. Skeat, Specimens of Early English, revised a second time, Part II (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1873), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; Proverbs of Hendyng pp. 35-42, Alysoun p. 43, A Plea for Pity pp. 44-45, The Labourers in the Vineyard pp. 46-47, and Spring-time pp. 48-49
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the five target overlaps and exact pages, including three reading-level comparisons in Böddeker’s apparatus.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright and James Orchard Halliwell, eds., Reliquiae Antiquae, vol. 1 (London: John Russell Smith, 1845), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Marian comparator from Egerton 613, pp. 102-103; Harley Proverbs of Hendyng, pp. 109-116; Digby 86 Hendyng excerpt, pp. 256-257
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the two item-scoped comparator groups Böddeker cites and to keep their named witness contexts separate from his direct Harley base.

evidence.citation: Thomas Wright, ed., The Latin Poems Commonly Attributed to Walter Mapes (London: Camden Society, 1841), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Body and Soul comparator versions pp. 334-345; Harley 2253 appendix text pp. 346-349
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the Body and Soul passages Böddeker cites from Wright, including the precise Harley appendix location.

evidence.citation: Eduard Mall, The Harrowing of Hell: Das altenglische Spiel von Christi Höllenfahrt (Breslau: Maruschke & Berendt, 1871), complete Google/Internet Archive PDF and official DDB record.
evidence.locator: Complete 56-page critical edition and variant apparatus; title/publication details; BSB URN urn:nbn:de:bvb:12-bsb10746686-7; embedded Google usage notice
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify the edition Böddeker relied on for Harley, Auchinleck, and Digby variants. It supports only the printed-intermediary relation, not direct Böddeker access to the latter two manuscripts.

evidence.citation: John Mitchell Kemble, ed., The Dialogue of Salomon and Saturnus (London: Ælfric Society, 1848), complete Internet Archive PDF.
evidence.locator: Title page and contents; Proverbs of Hending, printed pp. 270-282
evidence.summary: Complete scan used to verify Kemble’s Harley 2253 text and the exact Hendyng pages Böddeker consulted.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of copyright (current guidance).
evidence.locator: Pre-1989 unpublished works (2039 rule) and common questions
evidence.summary: Current official guidance explains that some literary, dramatic, and musical works unpublished before 1989 can remain protected until 31 December 2039 even when their authors died centuries ago; publication and public-availability history therefore require item-specific analysis.

open_questions.question: What current item-specific route and rights statement apply to the U-M OCR and reported 484 page images?
next_steps: Verify the stable resolver in an ordinary browser or contact U-M Digital Collections.
Request image identifiers, conversion/version history, and source-specific rights terms.
Do not cache Cloudflare challenge pages or treat the IA metadata-only item as content.

open_questions.question: Can a lawful complete digital copy of Ker’s 1965 facsimile, or authorized current images of all target Harley folios, be obtained with clear reuse terms?
next_steps: Submit a British Library complete-object imaging enquiry with every physical surface and a separate target-folio map.
Ask EETS and holding libraries about licensed access to OS 255.
Recheck the BL catalog when its advertised full image coverage is restored.

open_questions.question: Which earlier printed readings or parallel witnesses did Böddeker adopt in each of the forty-five selected texts?
next_steps: Audit Böddeker’s item-level notes against each cited edition and the available manuscript images.
Record adoption, consultation, and collation relations only at the affected item scope.
Do not imply direct Böddeker consultation of Digby 86; his own introduction credits E. Stengel.

open_questions.question: How should U-M’s embedded restriction notice and current unavailable item rights be reconciled with the CME repository’s software-only Unlicense for redistribution of afy7793.xml?
next_steps: Seek written source-specific clarification from U-M and the CME maintainer.
Keep the medieval text, Böddeker edition, scan derivatives, OCR/markup, and repository file as separate rights layers.

open_questions.question: What item-by-item publication or lawful public-availability history determines the United Kingdom copyright status of the forty-five medieval texts?
next_steps: Audit each item’s pre-1989 publication and public-availability history, including Böddeker and the modeled earlier printed editions.
Apply current Intellectual Property Office guidance to each item rather than inferring United Kingdom status from medieval age or United States public-domain status.
Keep modern transcriptions, editorial matter, photographs, and provider terms as separate rights layers.

review_notes: Cataloging subject is the exact forty-five-text edited selection, not the complete 1878 book or manuscript.

All eighteen cached files are hardlinked between the isolated worktree and main source-cache/afy7793; DIMEV v1, Mätzner 1867, Reliquiae Antiquae volume 1, and Wright’s Latin Poems also share canonical existing inodes used by other works.

Every local copy has an explicit target_work_presence: sixteen content-bearing files are present with reproducible work_portion locators; the PIMS scan-data and U-M IA metadata files are absent with no work_portion.
Ten prior printed editions and their ten digital carriers are modeled separately; Böddeker’s item-scoped consulted/collated edges remain lateral to his direct transcription of Harley 2253.
No anti-bot response, incomplete target surrogate, or unverified manuscript-image endpoint is cached.
The modern METS and DIMEV sources are collateral controls, not upstream sources of afy7793.
